ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
ale: noun. A beer made without hops.

eat: verb. To ingest; to be ingested.
equal: adjective. The same in one or more respects.
equality: noun. The fact of being equal.
equity: noun. Fairness, impartiality, or justice as determined in light of " natural law " or "natural right ".

laity: noun. People of a church who are not ordained clergy or clerics.
